The House of Love was a television variety show starring Jayne Mansfield that aired on ABC from 1960 to 1962. The show was produced by Mansfield's husband, Jack Webb, and featured a mix of comedy, music, and dance performances. The show was a hit with audiences and helped to establish Mansfield as a major Hollywood star.

The show premiered on September 22, 1960, and ended on May 14, 1962. During its run, the show featured a variety of guest stars, including Elvis Presley, Dean Martin, and Frank Sinatra. The show also featured Mansfield's own musical act, which included a mix of pop and rock and roll songs.

The House of Love was a popular show during the 1960s, and it helped to establish Jayne Mansfield as a major Hollywood star. The show was a hit with audiences and helped to establish Mansfield as a major Hollywood star.

<answer>September 22, 1960 to May 14, 1962</answer>
